name: Middle Woodford
overview: Middle Woodford is the central of the three villages - with Lower and Upper Woodford - that make up Woodford civil parish, strung along the west bank of the Salisbury Avon about 5 miles north of Salisbury. The parish had a population of 443 at the 2011 census. The Grade II listed parish church of All Saints, with 12th-century origins and restored in 1845 by T.H. Wyatt, stands here, as does the Woodford Valley village hall that serves all three villages.
character: A tranquil, entirely rural chalk-stream valley village with no main road running through it, set among willow-lined water meadows beside the river.
amenities: Woodford Valley village hall (quiz nights, coffee mornings, a market and cafe, and a mobile library)
Salisbury Reds bus service through the valley
No village shop or supermarket - everyday shopping is in Salisbury

commutes.notes: About 5 miles south down the valley road for shops, services and the station
From Salisbury on South Western Railway; fastest services about 1h 20m
Salisbury Reds runs through the Woodford Valley villages

prices.summary: Housing is mostly period cottages, thatched and brick-and-flint houses and converted farm buildings, with very low transaction volumes given the small size of the village; the riverside chalk-valley setting and proximity to Salisbury support premium values. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: Visiting Heale Garden at Heale House, between Middle and Upper Woodford, where Charles II sheltered in 1651 after the Battle of Worcester
Walking and fishing along the River Avon water meadows
placesToEat: The Bridge Inn, a riverside pub at neighbouring Upper Woodford
The Wheatsheaf Inn at Lower Woodford
Market and cafe events at the Woodford Valley village hall
pros: Exceptionally peaceful chalk-valley setting with no through traffic
Well-regarded village Church of England primary academy on the doorstep
Only about 5 miles from Salisbury's full range of shops, services and rail links
cons: No shop or supermarket in the village, so a car is essential
Limited bus frequency compared with town living
Some riverside properties sit close to the Avon flood plain
whoItSuits: Families and downsizers wanting a quiet, picturesque riverside village within easy reach of Salisbury.

sources.label: Ofsted - Woodford Valley CE Primary Academy (URN 138188, Good, Jun 2025)
H&W White - the Woodford Valley villages (amenities, bus and rail)
